 {"id":"bd-1d3","title":"Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure","description":"# Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure

## Goal
Create infrastructure for testing the interactive TUI by capturing terminal output
and comparing against golden snapshots, with detailed logging.

## Background
src/interactive.rs is 2842 lines with only 2 trivial tests (<0.1% coverage).
This is the most user-visible code and needs comprehensive testing.

## Approach: Snapshot Testing

### Why Snapshots?
- TUI output is complex (ANSI codes, layout)
- Pixel-perfect testing impractical
- Snapshots capture "expected output" simply
- Easy to update when intentional changes made

### Snapshot Format
```
// tests/snapshots/tui_initial_state.snap
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pi - AI Coding Agent                │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                     │
│ > [cursor]                          │
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Model: claude-sonnet-4   Tokens: 0  │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each snapshot test.
- Log view size, theme name, state flags, and snapshot name.
- On mismatch, dump the rendered view (ANSI-stripped) + diff context.

## Determinism Requirements
- Force terminal size (80x24) and disable time-based animations/spinners in test mode.
- Strip ANSI codes for snapshots.
- Use a fixed default theme and locale.

## Implementation

### Snapshot Library
Use insta crate for Rust snapshot testing:
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
insta = { version = "1", features = ["filters"] }
```

### Test Structure
```rust
// tests/tui_snapshot.rs

use insta::assert_snapshot;
use pi::interactive::PiApp;

#[test]
fn test_initial_state() {
    let app = PiApp::new(Config::default());
    let view = app.view();
    assert_snapshot!("initial_state", strip_ansi(&view));
}
```

### ANSI Code Handling
Strip ANSI codes for deterministic snapshots:
```rust
fn strip_ansi(s: &str) -> String {
    let re = regex::Regex::new(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]").unwrap();
    re.replace_all(s, "").to_string()
}
```

### Width/Height Normalization
Force consistent terminal size:
```rust
fn normalized_view(app: &PiApp) -> String {
    app.view_with_size(80, 24)
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### Layout Tests
1. Initial empty state
2. Single user message
3. Single assistant message
4. Conversation with multiple messages
5. Long message with wrapping
6. Scrolled viewport

### State Tests
7. Idle state
8. Streaming text
9. Streaming thinking
10. Tool execution in progress
11. Error display
12. Slash command help

### Input Tests
13. Text in input field
14. Multi-line input
15. History navigation
16. Cursor positioning

## Dependencies
None (can start independently)

## Files
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs
- tests/snapshots/*.snap (generated)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] insta crate integrated
- [ ] ANSI stripping works
- [ ] Terminal size normalized
- [ ] 15+ snapshot tests
- [ ] Logs include snapshot metadata
- [ ] cargo insta test works
- [ ] CI validates snapshots","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:28.554490342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T08:42:46.796475700Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:42:46.796412182Z","close_reason":"Added tui_snapshot tests + insta snapshots; suite green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-1d3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: added TUI snapshot test harness (tests/tui_snapshot.rs) using TestHarness + insta; added PiApp::set_terminal_size and PI_TEST_MODE init gating for animations; added dev-dep insta. Snapshots pending because cargo check/clippy failing due to removed reqwest/tokio deps still referenced in code; rustfmt still fails on import ordering in src/interactive.rs and provider tests.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:19:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1d5i","title":"E2E Interactive: /settings UI toggles + persistence","description":"# Goal
End-to-end interactive test for `/settings` UI toggles and persistence.

# Scope
- Launch interactive session using the tmux harness from `bd-3hp` (fixed 80x24 capture).
- Toggle key settings (quietStartup, collapseChangelog, hideThinkingBlock, cursor, padding).
- Exit and relaunch to verify settings persisted and applied.

# Logging
- Capture tmux frames, stdout/stderr, and settings.json before/after.
- Log each toggle action and expected UI effect.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic, offline script with artifact-rich logs.
- Clear assertions on persisted settings and visual state.

# Dependencies
- `bd-3hp` tmux capture harness.
- `/settings` parity + persistence (`bd-axuu`).
- Unified JSONL logging spec (`bd-4u9`).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:59:53.118871512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:00:05.603988626Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1d5i","depends_on_id":"bd-3hp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T05:59:48.493598770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d5i","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:59:53.134545139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d5i","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:59:53.140602978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d5i","depends_on_id":"bd-c4q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:59:53.129977558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dd3","title":"Interactive: render /hotkeys from active keybindings","description":"# Goal
Replace the static `/hotkeys` output with a dynamically generated view from the active keybindings.

# Required Behavior
- `/hotkeys` should list shortcuts grouped by category, mirroring the legacy docs (Cursor Movement, Deletion, Text Input, Application, Session, Models & Thinking, Display, Message Queue, Selection).
- If user overrides are loaded, `/hotkeys` must reflect them.

# Nice-to-have (but keep scope tight)
- Show both default and overridden keys (e.g., dim default, bright override).
- Include a hint for where to edit: `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/hotkeys` is generated from action catalog + bindings.
- [ ] Output is stable and testable (snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:55.925113647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:42.591931473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:42.591862805Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3qm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-cru","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"/hotkeys dynamic rendering implementation complete (bd-1dd3).

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**

1. **Added `format_hotkeys()` method:**
   - Iterates through all action categories using `ActionCategory::all()`
   - Groups actions by category with display headers
   - Shows key bindings from the active keybindings (supports user overrides)
   - Includes config file path for user reference
   - Skips empty categories (actions without bindings)

2. **Updated SlashCommand::Hotkeys handler:**
   - Replaced static string with dynamic `self.format_hotkeys()` call
   - Output now reflects actual active keybindings including user overrides

**Output format:**
```
Keyboard Shortcuts
==================

Config: ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json

## Cursor Movement

  up                   Move cursor up
  down                 Move cursor down
  left, ctrl+b         Move cursor left
  ...

## Application

  escape               Cancel / abort
  ctrl+c               Clear editor
  ...
```

**Test added (tests/tui_state.rs):**
- `tui_state_slash_hotkeys_shows_dynamic_keybindings` - verifies output contains bindings and descriptions

**Tests: All 51 TUI tests passing, 207+ total**

-{"id":"bd-1e0","title":"Implement extension discovery + install resolution","description":"Background:
- pi CLI must discover extensions from packages and CLI flags; this is core UX.

Steps:
- Extend ResourceLoader/PackageManager to resolve extension assets and manifests.
- Respect CLI flags (--extension/--no-extensions) and precedence rules (project > global).
- Produce structured diagnostics for missing/invalid/conflicting extensions.
- Ensure resolution is deterministic and cache-safe for repeatable tests.

Logging requirements:
- Diagnostics include source, resolution path, and reason codes.

Acceptance:
- Extensions can be installed, discovered, and enumerated from settings + CLI.
- Diagnostics are actionable and stable for unit/E2E assertions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:58.695313308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:46.385800663Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-3sf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-gqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1f5","title":"Extensions: QuickJS runtime + Pi event loop (no Node/Bun)","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires deterministic event loop semantics without Node/Bun.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: wasm-using JS bundles must use the PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) or Tier A WASM components.

Steps:
- Embed QuickJS via safe Rust bindings and expose a minimal Pi event loop.
- Implement microtask + promise ordering and timer scheduling per bd-123.
- Provide a hostcall bridge: JS promises resolve/reject only via the connector dispatcher (timeouts/cancel/policy errors correctly mapped).
- Load/inject required compatibility shims (Node core modules + globals) via the extc pipeline + module loader contract.
- Emit structured logs for JS task scheduling, hostcalls, policy decisions, and wasm-bridge activity.

Acceptance (hard):
- JS-tier runtime can execute the **full pinned sample set** where applicable: **16/16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run with no manual source edits**, using shims/rewrites/bridge as needed.
- Deterministic ordering is testable and reproducible under harness control.

Notes:
- Node core module shims are tracked in bd-3d0 (non-child_process) and bd-2sr (child_process).
- WebAssembly-in-JS is tracked in bd-1ry.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:24.611922334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:02:24.223037290Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting work on bd-1f5. Plan: review existing extension runtime scaffolding (src/extensions.rs, src/interactive.rs, src/resources.rs), identify integration points for a QuickJS host/event loop, and draft initial design + module skeleton. If anyone is already working on QuickJS bindings, please flag conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:54:15Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: added QuickJS runtime scaffolding in src/extensions_js.rs (AsyncRuntime/AsyncContext creation, script eval helpers, job draining, and stub pi.* hostcalls). Added clippy allowance for non-Send futures. Tests: cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:06:57Z"},{"id":211,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"BlackOwl","text":"Picking up bd-1f5 build break: src/extensions_js.rs SWC/TS transpile code doesn’t compile against current swc_* crate APIs (resolver/strip/new_source_file/etc). Plan: fix API usage, remove any leftover debug prints in rpc tests, then run cargo fmt/check/clippy/test. FYI I saw Agent Mail file reservations on src/extensions_js.rs / tests/rpc_mode.rs (RainyStone, RusticBadger) and requested contact to coordinate.","created_at":"2026-02-04T05:31:45Z"},{"id":215,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"BlackOwl","text":"Progress: repo back to green. Fixed clippy/warnings in src/extensions_js.rs (resolver lifetime elision, match arm merge, redundant clone, Path-based tsx detection, allow too_many_lines for virtual module stubs, base64 encode uses u8::try_from). Cleaned unused imports in src/extensions.rs. Minor rpc.rs match arm tidy. Tests: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-04T06:02:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fg","title":"Implement extension benchmarks (load, tool call, event hook)","description":"Background:
- Need measurable evidence for runtime overhead.

Steps:
- Add benchmarks for extension load/init, tool call roundtrip, event hook emission.
- Ensure benchmarks can run with a representative extension artifact.
- Record results in BENCHMARKS.md.

Acceptance:
- `cargo bench` runs extension benchmarks and outputs stable numbers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:47.669514632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:45.449698662Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-1ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-1pb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-7cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fq","title":"Integration tests: session picker resume flow","description":"# Goal
Add integration tests for `src/session_picker.rs` and `Session::resume_with_picker`, including TTY vs non‑TTY fallbacks.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Create multiple sessions, verify picker ordering by last_modified.
- Validate resume with explicit selection and correct session path.
- Non‑TTY fallback: auto‑create new session (no prompt).
- Empty session directory fallback: new session created with correct base dir.
- Ensure session index is used when available (else scan).

# Determinism
- Use temp dirs + deterministic timestamps; avoid asserting wall‑clock values.
- Use stdout capture for picker output assertions.

# Acceptance
- 8+ integration tests covering picker selection and fallback paths.
- Logs include session paths and selection inputs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:19.082952750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:29:27.760822073Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fq","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fq","depends_on_id":"bd-2rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":158,"issue_id":"bd-1fq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Use temp sessions with controlled mtimes to verify picker ordering.
- Validate non‑TTY path by forcing stdin/stdout to non‑tty (use test harness).
- Ensure index path vs fallback scan code paths are both exercised.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:08Z"}]}

Prevent regressions by asserting that the most important errors render with the expected hint(s).

 {"id":"bd-1zqk","title":"Docs: releasing.md (versioning + publish + release checklist)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/releasing.md` so the release process is explicit, repeatable, and does not require reading CI YAML.

# Must Include
- Version/tag policy (link to `Release: versioning + tag strategy`)
- How to generate/update `CHANGELOG.md`
- How to publish crates (if applicable) and how it relates to tags
- How to publish GitHub Releases binaries
- Pre-release checklist:
  - CI green on main
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --all-targets`
  - benchmarks (if required for this release)
- Post-release checklist:
  - verify artifacts
  - verify install instructions

# Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-20c","title":"Migrate core runtime to asupersync (agent/tools/session)","description":"Background:
- Provider HTTP migration (bd-1xf) is necessary but not sufficient to remove tokio.
- Core runtime still uses tokio for agent loop, channels, tool execution, and session IO.

Scope / Steps:
1) Replace tokio runtime bootstrap in main.rs with asupersync RuntimeBuilder + reactor.
2) Migrate agent loop + tool execution to Cx-based async; replace tokio channels with asupersync equivalents.
3) Ensure session IO + indexing use asupersync-friendly APIs (no tokio traits).
4) Update tests to use LabRuntime for deterministic time/cancellation.
5) Remove tokio dependency once all call sites migrated.

Acceptance:
- pi runs end-to-end without tokio in Cargo.toml.
- Cancellation + cleanup behave correctly across agent/tool/session paths.
- Tests pass under LabRuntime.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.867634353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:36:58.337819453Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:36:58.337751026Z","close_reason":"Audit: tokio not present (rg no matches); main uses asupersync RuntimeBuilder/create_reactor; core modules use asupersync channels.  {"id":"bd-26s","title":"Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)

## Goal
Achieve high-confidence coverage using real code paths wherever possible, paired with detailed E2E logging. “No mocks” here means: no fake providers or stubbed tool results on core paths; local deterministic test servers are allowed only with an explicit allowlist + rationale.

## Current State (2026-02-04)
- **Strong coverage exists**: tools conformance + session conformance, SSE parser, model serialization, config tests, TUI snapshots/state, CLI selection, and multiple module-local unit tests.
- **Mocks/fakes still present**: `rpc_mode` tests use `TestProvider`; local `MockHttp` servers in harness; fake `gh` scripts in TUI tests; ext_conformance fixtures include `mock_openai`.
- **VCR harness exists**, but cassette recording is incomplete for provider streaming.

## Remaining Gaps / Active Fixes (bead-linked)
- Provider VCR cassette capture + streaming event validation: `bd-30u`, `bd-11k`, `bd-h7r`.
- Replace `TestProvider` in RPC with VCR playback: `bd-17o`, `bd-kh2`.
- Unified JSONL logging + artifact index (blocked on normalization): `bd-4u9` ← `bd-3s2`.
- Unit/integration no-mock closures: `bd-102` children (e.g., `bd-3f0o`, `bd-16kl`, `bd-2q2v`, `bd-3n53`, `bd-3q6z`, `bd-3pn`, `bd-2b3`, `bd-p2l`, `bd-gd1`).
- E2E CLI/TUI/tool scripts with detailed logs: `bd-c4q` children (`bd-27t`, `bd-20t3`, `bd-2fz9`, `bd-2z22`, `bd-2xyv`, `bd-1d5i`, `bd-19th`, `bd-3hp`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All `bd-102` unit/integration children closed with real-path tests.
- All `bd-c4q` E2E children closed and logs conform to `bd-4u9` schema.
- RPC tests use VCR-backed streams (no `TestProvider`).
- Coverage matrix updated (via `bd-1nn`) with explicit mock allowlist + rationale.

 {"id":"bd-28rk","title":"Autocomplete: core provider (commands/prompts/skills/files/paths)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core autocomplete provider that produces suggestions based on editor text + cursor position.

# Suggestions required (legacy parity)
- Slash commands: built-in commands (`/help`, `/model`, `/settings`, etc.).
- Prompt templates: `/<template>` from ResourceLoader.
- Skills: `/skill:<name>` when enableSkillCommands is true.
- File reference: when user types `@`, fuzzy-search project files and insert selected path.
- Path completion: when cursor is in a path-like token, produce candidates (use `fd` if available).

# Matching
- Fuzzy matching for file and command lists.
- Stable ordering (deterministic): exact prefix matches first, then fuzzy score.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep provider logic independent of rendering.
- Provider should return a structured `AutocompleteItem`:
  - display label
  - insert text
  - optional description
  - kind (command/template/skill/file/path)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provider can be called with (text, cursor) and returns stable suggestions.
- [ ] Provider includes file suggestions without scanning the entire filesystem on every keystroke (cache or incremental search).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.177076040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441887518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441818900Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs
- tests/common/logging.rs
- tests/common/harness.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger captures all levels
- [ ] Context key-values supported
- [ ] Timestamps included
- [ ] Redaction helpers present
- [ ] Artifact registry present
- [ ] Auto-dump on test failure
- [ ] Optional file output works
- [ ] All integration tests use harness","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.070756084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124493922Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124430233Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction helpers, artifact registry + dump, and TEST_LOG_PATH file output in test logging; harness now dumps artifacts on panic and provides record_artifact. Acceptance criteria satisfied.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ml","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-3ml","author":"QuietBear","text":"Reviewed existing tests/common logger/harness: core logging already present but missing redaction, artifact registry, and TEST_LOG_PATH dump. Implementing those now in tests/common/logging.rs + tests/common/harness.rs (record_artifact, dump_artifacts, redaction helper, env-based file output, artifact dump on panic).","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3n53","title":"Unit: RPC protocol schema + tool routing invariants","description":"# Goal
Add no-mock unit tests for RPC protocol encoding/decoding and tool routing invariants.

# Scope
- Validate request/response JSON schema parsing (good + malformed payloads).
- Ensure tool call routing preserves ids, names, and argument shapes.
- Error mapping: invalid method, missing params, unexpected tool result types.
- Serialization round-trips for key RPC structs.

# Logging
- Use TestLogger to dump failing payload fragments and expected vs actual variants.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/rpc_mode.rs` or a new `tests/rpc_protocol.rs`.
- No network; deterministic fixtures only.
- Clear assertions on error variants and message shapes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:29.253144513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:03:27.614806457Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:02:19.695150625Z","close_reason":"Completed: added rpc protocol tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n53","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:29.274215127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":214,"issue_id":"bd-3n53","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented RPC protocol unit tests in  (invalid JSON, missing type, unknown command, missing params, and get_messages tool-call identity/args invariants). -{"id":"bd-3nix","title":"Workstream: /reload Parity (resources + autocomplete + diagnostics)","description":"# Goal
Bring `/reload` behavior to legacy parity.

# Legacy Behavior
In pi-mono (`interactive-mode.ts`), `/reload`:
- reloads the session resources
- applies themes
- rebuilds autocomplete
- (if extensions enabled) sets up extension shortcuts
- rebuilds chat from messages
- shows loaded resources summary
- surfaces models.json errors

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/reload` calls `ResourceLoader::load(...)` and reports counts.
- It does not:
  - rebuild autocomplete
  - show models.json parse errors
  - reload context files (AGENTS)
  - reload extensions (beyond current extension workstream)

# Scope / Deliverables
1) After reload, update interactive state:
  - skills/prompts/themes collections
  - slash/template/skill command registry
  - any header “loaded resources” display

2) Rebuild autocomplete sources (depends on autocomplete workstream).

3) Surface diagnostics:
  - models.json error (from `ModelRegistry::error()`)
  - resource loader diagnostics

4) Extensions reload integration is explicitly **blocked** by ongoing extensions work; do not step on that surface.

# Testing
- State tests for `/reload` showing correct counts and applying new resources.
- Unit tests for diagnostic formatting.

# Dependencies
- Autocomplete workstream (`bd-1iwi`) for rebuilding autocomplete.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] /reload updates resources in running session
[ ] Autocomplete sources rebuild after reload
[ ] models.json and resource diagnostics surfaced
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] /reload updates resources in running session
[ ] Autocomplete sources rebuild after reload
[ ] models.json and resource diagnostics surfaced","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:46:50.063763059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:00:17.943490507Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nix","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] persist() calls index_session()
- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist
- [ ] Performance impact <10ms","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.093344392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:26.066864853Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:06.998331380Z","close_reason":"Completed: Session::save() now updates SessionIndex (best-effort); index stored under sessions root; redundant index_session calls removed; test added.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nz","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-3nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picking up bd-3nz. Plan: make Session::save() update SessionIndex (best-effort: warn-only, never fails save), move index DB under the sessions root for easy isolation, and remove redundant SessionIndex::index_session calls from AgentSession + interactive save paths. Touching: src/session.rs, src/session_index.rs, src/agent.rs, src/interactive.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:06:32Z"},{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-3nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed and verified: Session::save() updates the per-sessions-root SQLite index (warn-only on failure), SessionIndex is rooted under the sessions directory, and callers no longer manually index after save. Cargo gates passed: cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; cargo test.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oa9","title":"Docs: troubleshooting.md (common failures + fixes)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/troubleshooting.md` so users can self-serve common problems without scanning source or CI logs.

# Source Material
- Start from the existing Troubleshooting section in `README.md`.
- Add Rust-port-specific topics (VCR mode, packages, sessions, keybindings).

# Must Include
- API key issues (env vars vs settings vs `--api-key`)
- Provider errors (401/429/5xx) and what Pi does (retry, messaging)
- VCR playback/recording behavior for tests
- Missing system deps (`fd`, `rg`) and install commands
- Session issues (where sessions live, how to recover, what corruption means)
- Terminal quirks (keybindings, paste, Windows Terminal notes)

# Linkage
- Where behavior is tracked by a bead, link the bead ID.
- Keep this doc aligned with the Error UX workstream (hints should match docs wording).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/troubleshooting.md` exists and is referenced from README.
- [ ] Common failure modes have concrete, copy-pastable fixes.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:24:28.463280562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:55:34.077289354Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T02:55:34.077223722Z","close_reason":"Added docs/troubleshooting.md and linked from README","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oa9","depends_on_id":"bd-1iz5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oa9","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
